The safe mitigation action 'switch-to-pump-2' was selected to shift the load and resolve the issue.

A pressure transient was detected downstream of the pump manifold, followed by an unexplained increase in flow in the north-zone and a simultaneous drop in main-line pressure and rise in pump current. These symptoms, as documented in the leak runbook, strongly indicate a leak in the main-line segment. The safe mitigation action 'isolate-main-line-segment' was selected to close valves around the suspected leak.

A pressure transient was detected downstream of the pump manifold, followed by an unexplained increase in flow in the north-zone. Telemetry showed a simultaneous drop in main pressure and a rise in pump current. These symptoms, combined with operator notes regarding telemetry drift, strongly indicate a leak in the main-line. The recommended mitigation is to isolate the main-line segment by closing valves.

A significant drop in main-line pressure (from ~500 to ~373 kPa) coincided with an increase in north-zone flow and a rise in pump-1 current. These symptoms, combined with operator notes and the runbook for leaks, strongly indicate a leak in the main-line segment. The safe mitigation 'isolate-main-line-segment' was selected to close valves around the suspected leak.
